dataset.title: Dataset of Experimental Non-Standard Dynamic Hysteresis Loops

interpretation: A useful QA hysteresis mapping should improve held-out loss prediction over the mean baseline and should be evaluated against Steinmetz. The rank-only QA models test whether measured loop geometry maps into QA coordinates without physical scale. The dimensioned QA shell model keeps calibration-set H/B shell centers and directly approximates the hysteresis work integral.

orbit_bridge_summary.orbit_bridge_permutation_interpretation: Observed canonical orbit-family transfer wins: 6/12. Shuffled-label null summary: orbit_family_shuffle_null: p=0.510, max_wins=9; norm_f_shuffle_null: p=0.105, max_wins=7; family_norm_joint_shuffle_null: p=0.225, max_wins=8. The observed signal is not cleanly above all shuffled-label nulls; interpret the bridge accordingly.
orbit_bridge_summary.orbit_bridge_result_interpretation: Earlier ad hoc QA summaries failed to produce a stable advantage over ordinary calibrated H/B shell physics. Canonical QA orbit topology is the first established QA substrate in this experiment that matches and slightly beats the non-QA shell baseline on the main split: orbit-family residual delta RMSE=-0.009236 mJ/kg and delta R2=0.000047. The transfer result is promising but not conclusive at 6/12 RMSE wins. The norm_f and branch/lag variants are reported separately because adding them is not automatically better. The next hardening step is a significance/permutation test over orbit labels or bootstrap over loops.

physical_observable.loop_integral_check: abs(integral H dB) / density converted to mJ/kg

observables: abs(integral H_bin dB_bin) converted to mJ/kg
abs(integral H_shell dB_shell) converted to mJ/kg
abs(integral H_J dB_X) converted to mJ/kg
abs(integral H_K dB_X) converted to mJ/kg
abs(integral H_(K-J) dB_X) converted to mJ/kg
abs(integral H_J dB_Pi) converted to mJ/kg
closed-loop transition graph observables over state_t=(b_t,e_t)
branch-local lookup reconstruction from QA contexts to H/B shell paths
qa_mapping.qa_state_centers: global calibration-loop joint (b,e) state means only

transfer_split_summary.memory_result_interpretation: Memory-only QA wins 1 of 12 RMSE transfer splits against non-QA shell; memory plus QA shell wins 2; non-QA shell plus QA memory wins 2; QA memory plus transition wins 0; non-QA shell plus transition plus memory wins 2. Best memory family: qa_memory_plus_qa_shell.
transfer_split_summary.orbit_residual_result_interpretation: Established QA orbit-topology residual reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 6/12 splits for orbit family, 2/12 for orbit family + norm_f mod 24, and 5/12 for orbit family + norm + branch/lag. Best orbit residual family: qa_orbit_residual_reconstruct_family_direct.
transfer_split_summary.reconstruction_result_interpretation: Branch-local lookup reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 0/12 splits for (b,e), 0/12 for (b,e,sb,se), 0/12 for (b,e,branch,lag), and 0/12 for full memory context. Best reconstruction family: qa_reconstruct_be_direct. Because the declared reconstruction target is the marginal H/B shell center and every tested context contains (b,e), this formulation collapses to ordinary shell reconstruction rather than adding QA-specific branch information.
transfer_split_summary.residual_reconstruction_result_interpret: Residual branch-local reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 2/12 splits for (b,e), 3/12 for (b,e,sb,se), 3/12 for (b,e,branch,lag), and 3/12 for full memory context. Best residual reconstruction family: qa_residual_reconstruct_be_dir_direct.

verdict.leakage_controls: H/B quantile edges, marginal shell centers, and joint QA state centers are fit on calibration loops only. Direct predictors use no held-out loss labels and no fitted regression coefficient. Branch-local reconstruction lookup tables are fit separately per split on calibration traces only. Residual reconstruction lookup tables also use calibration traces only and fall back to (b,e) residuals or zero residual when a held-out context is unseen.
verdict.lifted_pair_sweep_interpretation: Best lifted pair by the predeclared transfer score is b/X, with RMSE wins in 4 of 12 splits and median RMSE ratio 1.507 relative to ordinary non-QA shell. This does not establish a stable QA-specific advantage yet.
verdict.memory_result_interpretation: Memory-only QA wins 1 of 12 RMSE transfer splits against non-QA shell; memory plus QA shell wins 2; non-QA shell plus QA memory wins 2; QA memory plus transition wins 0; non-QA shell plus transition plus memory wins 2. Best memory family: qa_memory_plus_qa_shell.
verdict.orbit_bridge_permutation_interpretation: Observed canonical orbit-family transfer wins: 6/12. Shuffled-label null summary: orbit_family_shuffle_null: p=0.510, max_wins=9; norm_f_shuffle_null: p=0.105, max_wins=7; family_norm_joint_shuffle_null: p=0.225, max_wins=8. The observed signal is not cleanly above all shuffled-label nulls; interpret the bridge accordingly.
verdict.orbit_bridge_result_interpretation: Earlier ad hoc QA summaries failed to produce a stable advantage over ordinary calibrated H/B shell physics. Canonical QA orbit topology is the first established QA substrate in this experiment that matches and slightly beats the non-QA shell baseline on the main split: orbit-family residual delta RMSE=-0.009236 mJ/kg and delta R2=0.000047. The transfer result is promising but not conclusive at 6/12 RMSE wins. The norm_f and branch/lag variants are reported separately because adding them is not automatically better. The next hardening step is a significance/permutation test over orbit labels or bootstrap over loops.
verdict.orbit_residual_result_interpretation: Established QA orbit-topology residual reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 6/12 splits for orbit family, 2/12 for orbit family + norm_f mod 24, and 5/12 for orbit family + norm + branch/lag. Best orbit residual family: qa_orbit_residual_reconstruct_family_direct.
verdict.phase_pair_sweep_interpretation: Best phase/orientation-aware QA pair is b/X, with RMSE wins in 4 of 12 splits and median RMSE ratio 1.453 relative to ordinary non-QA shell.
verdict.qa_adds_to_steinmetz_here: The tested log Steinmetz+rank-QA shape model does not improve Steinmetz, but the physically dimensioned QA shell integral beats Steinmetz directly.
verdict.qa_beats_steinmetz_here: YES: the direct dimensioned QA shell integral outperforms the log-linear Steinmetz baseline on this selected Ring35 SIN subset.
verdict.qa_lifted_vs_nonqa_shell_here: MIXED: calibrated QA J/Pi shells have higher held-out R2 than ordinary H/B shell binning, but worse MAE/RMSE. This is not yet a clean QA-specific win.
verdict.qa_not_random: YES: QA-only held-out models reduce error substantially relative to the mean-loss baseline on real measured loops.
verdict.reconstruction_result_interpretation: Branch-local lookup reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 0/12 splits for (b,e), 0/12 for (b,e,sb,se), 0/12 for (b,e,branch,lag), and 0/12 for full memory context. Best reconstruction family: qa_reconstruct_be_direct. Because the declared reconstruction target is the marginal H/B shell center and every tested context contains (b,e), this formulation collapses to ordinary shell reconstruction rather than adding QA-specific branch information.
verdict.residual_reconstruction_result_interpret: Residual branch-local reconstruction wins against ordinary non-QA shell in 2/12 splits for (b,e), 3/12 for (b,e,sb,se), 3/12 for (b,e,branch,lag), and 3/12 for full memory context. Best residual reconstruction family: qa_residual_reconstruct_be_dir_direct.
verdict.shell_result_interpretation: The raw physical loop integral is the expected upper-bound sanity check. Ordinary non-QA H/B shell binning explains most of the direct shell result and beats the joint (b,e) QA state shell by MAE/RMSE. A lifted QA J/Pi shell slightly beats non-QA by R2 but not by MAE/RMSE, so this run supports finite-shell hysteresis mapping and only a mixed, not decisive, QA-specific advantage.
verdict.transfer_result_interpretation: Across 12 index/frequency/amplitude transfer splits, QA J/Pi beats ordinary non-QA shell by R2 in 3 splits and by RMSE in 3 splits. This hardens the conclusion by checking family-heldout transfer. The lifted-pair sweep reports whether another QA variable pair transfers more consistently.
verdict.transition_result_interpretation: Transition-only QA wins 0 of 12 RMSE transfer splits against non-QA shell; QA transition plus QA shell wins 2; non-QA shell plus QA transition wins 2. Best transition family: qa_transition_plus_shell.
